title: Evolution of Distributed Systems to Cloud Computing
description: A visual representation of the key distributed system models and technologies that enabled the development of modern cloud computing.
relationships: Cluster and Grid computing provided the foundational models for resource pooling and high-performance distributed processing
P2P networks demonstrated decentralized resource sharing
Virtualization technology enabled the abstraction and flexible allocation of hardware resources, a core cloud enabler
SOA and Web 2.0 provided the architectural and application models for delivering software as a service over the web
Utility Computing introduced the 'pay-as-you-go' economic model that evolved into cloud service billing
